# Brown2Green

> Turn your brownfield codebase into an AI-ready project with specialised skills, guardrails, and workflows.

## About

Brown2Green is a methodology for making legacy codebases work with AI coding tools. It provides a step-by-step process with specialised skills that give AI the right context for each task — because bad code that's been around long enough starts looking normal.

## Methodology

Six phases to AI readiness:

1. **Assess** — Map architecture, identify pain points, establish baseline
2. **Document** — Create documentation layer AI can navigate
3. **Guard** — Set up hooks and guardrails to prevent breakage
4. **Specialise** — Install focused skills for each task type
5. **Workflow** — Define repeatable quality pipelines
6. **Iterate** — Progressive improvement, cycle by cycle

## Skills

- **codebase-qa** — Answers questions about codebase architecture
- **impact-analyser** — Predicts blast radius before changes
- **feature-planner** — Produces file-by-file implementation plans
- **feature-implementer** — Writes files following project conventions
- **preflight** — Quality gate: lint, type-check, build
- **reviewer** — Reviews diffs with severity ratings
- **committer** — Logical commit breakdown
- **documenter** — Architecture docs with diagrams

## Workflows

- **Simple** (1-3 files): understand → impact → change → preflight → review → commit
- **Complex** (multi-layer): plan → implement → QA → review → commit → document

## Compatibility

Works with Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, and any AI coding tool that supports skills/instructions.

## Links

- [Website](https://brown2green.dev)
- [Sign up for free skills](https://brown2green.dev/#signup)
